title: Exact age and social-status terminology remain unresolved
source_observation: The poem presents Sudhamathi as a capable woman and close companion but current research has not fixed her numerical age or exact social category
risk: Casting or publicity could invent an age or silently classify her as servant friend or dependent
decision: Cast an adult performer and keep relationship terminology provisional until Tamil and historical review
rationale: Protects textual honesty performer safety and social accuracy
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender history; casting; safeguarding

title: Sudhamathi owns her survivor narrative
source_observation: She recounts separation danger abandonment and household collapse
risk: Her story could be shortened into exposition completed by other characters or used only to motivate Manimekalai
decision: Give her control over the pace detail and limits of testimony and preserve aftermath and continuing judgement
rationale: Prevents instrumentalising a survivor and gives the character narrative authority
required_review: Classical Tamil; trauma consultant; intimacy coordinator; screenplay

title: Sudhamathi is not a sidekick or exposition device
source_observation: She acts as companion witness planner and bearer of experience
risk: Compression could reduce her to warning comic relief messenger or devotional admirer
decision: Give her independent perceptions choices disagreements and consequences in every retained sequence
rationale: Preserves personhood and strengthens the film's ordinary human perspective

title: Vigilance is skilled perception not cowardice
source_observation: Her past gives practical knowledge of danger status routes and loss of witnesses
risk: Performance shorthand could make her nervous weak or irrational beside a spiritually courageous heroine
decision: Stage warning through observable evidence and concrete decisions while allowing uncertainty and error
rationale: Respects survival knowledge without making it infallible

title: Violence is represented through consequence and survival
source_observation: Sudhamathi's history contains coercive danger abandonment injury and poverty
risk: Flashbacks could eroticise pursuit centre the aggressor or use suffering as spectacle
decision: Prioritise disorientation decisions bodily and economic aftermath testimony and recovery under documented intimacy protocols
rationale: Aligns form with survivor ownership and avoids exploitative imagery
required_review: Intimacy coordinator; trauma consultant; director; editor; legal

title: Friendship includes disagreement and mutual protection
source_observation: Sudhamathi and Manimekalai share danger information trust and companionship
risk: Their relationship could become one-way service or unquestioning admiration
decision: Write reciprocal care humour correction conflict and moments where each woman permits the other to choose
rationale: Makes friendship dramatically active and avoids hierarchy by default
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender studies; screenplay; actor rehearsal

title: Protectiveness must not become control
source_observation: Sudhamathi often recognises danger earlier and may feel responsible after prior loss
risk: The adaptation could reward surveillance restriction or command as the only proof of care
decision: Allow strong warnings and practical intervention but preserve Manimekalai's agency and show Sudhamathi confronting the limits of prevention
rationale: Creates a mature friendship arc consistent with the project's autonomy principles
required_review: Screenplay; trauma review; gender studies; director

title: Her voice remains concrete credible and capable of humour
source_observation: Sudhamathi must communicate danger testimony and ordinary companionship
risk: A uniformly fearful or solemn voice would reduce her to trauma while comic-sidekick writing would trivialise her experience
decision: Use practical specificity under danger controlled authorship in testimony and private warmth or dry humour in safety
rationale: Makes development audible and preserves emotional range
required_review: Dialogue; actor preparation; classical Tamil; editor

title: Later appearances require explicit adaptation logging
source_observation: The poem may not follow Sudhamathi across every later geographical or philosophical episode
risk: The screenplay could silently make her accompany Manimekalai everywhere or invent historical actions as textual fact
decision: Label each post-Puhar expansion as interpretation and record its function evidence and continuity cost
rationale: Protects source traceability while allowing justified cinematic continuity
required_review: Classical Tamil; adaptation editor; continuity; screenplay

title: Her ending preserves a life beyond the heroine's function
source_observation: Sudhamathi's dramatic value extends beyond warning and explaining Manimekalai
risk: She could disappear once the protagonist enters sacred and philosophical material
decision: Provide a final beat showing her own relationship responsibility choice or continuing life without falsely inventing a complete textual destiny
rationale: Prevents narrative abandonment and honours the character's independent agency
required_review: Screenplay; classical Tamil; gender studies; editor}
